selectionPolicy: Sort READY work by priorityScore descending, then no blockers, smallest safe scope, validation availability, and lease compatibility.

backlogWork.title: Add first-class email send governance policy objects
backlogWork.scope: Model send classifications, sender profiles, delivery profiles, CAN-SPAM/commercial-vs-transactional type, suppression/publication policy, and send-log retention as explicit workflow controls.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not bypass existing suppression, unsubscribe, warmup, inbox safety, or content sanitization controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Send governance objects have tenant/workspace-scoped ownership and auditability.
Campaign launch references explicit send policy choices before handoff.
Deliverability and suppression controls remain fail-closed.
Focused tests cover missing, invalid, and cross-tenant policy usage.
backlogWork.nextAction: Design schema/API boundaries for send governance before implementation.

backlogWork.title: Deepen Contact Builder style data-designer governance
backlogWork.scope: Add explicit data-source provenance, relationship cardinality controls, deletion audit, sendable-key governance, and safer relationship/query preview behavior for data extensions and contact records.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not migrate historical Flyway files or weaken tenant/workspace scoping.
acceptanceCriteria: Relationship/cardinality changes are validated and audited.
Sendable-key and retention/deletion policy changes are tenant/workspace scoped.
Query preview and segment behavior document performance constraints.
Tests cover cross-workspace denial and invalid relationship changes.
backlogWork.nextAction: Decompose data-designer governance into migration-safe service and UI slices.

backlogWork.title: Expand Automation Studio activity orchestration safely
backlogWork.scope: Decompose support for file/trigger starts, extracts, script/webhook/send activities, dependency ordering, notifications, and richer run-history/error details.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not add arbitrary script execution or external storage credentials without a separate security design.
acceptanceCriteria: Unsupported activity types fail closed with clear validation.
Dependency ordering and run-history design is documented before new activity execution is added.
Security review exists for scripts, file transfer, webhooks, and external object storage.
Follow-up READY slices are split by activity family.
backlogWork.nextAction: Create implementation plan and split executable activity families before coding.

backlogWork.title: Add journey step analytics and experimentation depth
backlogWork.scope: Add journey step metrics, conversion goals, random cohort and A/B reporting, winning-path reporting, and anomaly surfacing around journey/campaign execution.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not claim causal attribution or anomaly accuracy without measured evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Journey step metrics and conversion goals have explicit event contracts.
Experiment reporting separates campaign experiments from journey path tests.
Anomaly language is evidence-based and avoids unsupported AI claims.
Validation plan covers tracking ingestion, rollups, and UI rendering.
backlogWork.nextAction: Design the event and rollup contract before implementation.

backlogWork.title: Verify external email provider capacity and sender reputation
backlogWork.scope: Verify provider API/SMTP limits, sender reputation, DNS authentication, warmup status, feedback-loop handling, and rate-control policy before production high-volume claims.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not claim inbox placement, provider capacity, or 10 lakh send readiness from repository-only checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Provider capacity evidence is dated and reviewed.
Sender/domain warmup and DNS authentication evidence is present.
Feedback loop, bounce, complaint, suppression, and rate-control handling are verified.
blockers: External provider and sender reputation evidence not present in repository
backlogWork.nextAction: Collect provider, DNS, feedback-loop, warmup, and reputation evidence before production send claims.

backlogWork.title: Implement governed draft-only AI content assistance foundation
backlogWork.scope: Add tenant/workspace-scoped AI policy and audit scaffolding for draft-only content assistance, including provider disclosure, prompt/data classification, output safety review, and human approval before send or publication.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not call external model APIs, add provider credentials, autopublish generated content, or claim Salesforce Einstein parity in this slice.
acceptanceCriteria: Tenant/workspace AI policy defines feature class, provider disclosure, allowed data classes, training stance, retention, opt-in/out, and kill switch.
Content assistance remains draft-only and requires human review before send or publication.
Audit records capture policy version, actor, data classes, prompt template version, output hash, and review decision without storing secrets.
Tests cover policy denial, missing tenant/workspace context, and no-review/autopublish rejection.
backlogWork.nextAction: Design the tenant/workspace AI policy and audit schema for draft-only content assistance before implementation.

backlogWork.title: Define governed predictive segment contract
backlogWork.scope: Define predictive segment data provenance, threshold, preview, approval, tenant isolation, and rollback controls before model-backed audience creation.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not infer or target protected/sensitive categories, weaken suppression/preference controls, or create sendable audiences without preview and approval.
acceptanceCriteria: Predictive segment contract declares allowed feature sources, data freshness, minimum counts, bias/drift checks, and protected-data exclusions.
Predicted segments expose modeled-vs-rule-derived status, reasons, preview counts, suppression impact, and rollback snapshot.
Audience-changing recommendations require human approval and tenant/workspace isolation tests.
Follow-up implementation scope is split from Contact Builder data-designer governance when needed.
backlogWork.nextAction: Design predictive segment governance around provenance, preview, approval, and rollback.

backlogWork.title: Define governed engagement frequency optimization contract
backlogWork.scope: Define frequency optimization lookback, variant, fatigue, confidence, and deliverability guardrail contract before recommendations can affect send cadence.
backlogWork.nonGoals: Do not increase frequency for suppressed, unsubscribed, complaint-prone, warmup-blocked, over-cap, or provider-blocked recipients. Do not claim deliverability lift without evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Frequency contract defines lookback window, required variants, low-data fallback, saturation categories, confidence bands, and recommended cap.
Fatigue, unsubscribe, complaint, bounce, suppression, warmup, provider health, and rate controls are fail-closed safety inputs.
Recommendations expose expected safety impact and require approval when cadence increases or risk is non-low.
Focused implementation tests are identified for suppressions, caps, provider blocking, and tenant/workspace isolation.
backlogWork.nextAction: Design the frequency optimization safety contract before implementation.

blockedWork.title: Collect strict production release evidence
blockedWork.scope: Collect real target evidence for release promotion.
blockedWork.nonGoals: Do not replace production evidence with local-only checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Image, GA, egress, restore, monitoring, TLS/admission, CI/security, and load evidence are present.
Strict release gate passes without -LocalOnly.
blockers: External evidence not present in repository
blockedWork.nextAction: Collect external evidence, then run strict release gate.

blockedWork.title: Collect target-like high-volume send proof
blockedWork.scope: Prove 10 lakh send readiness with warmed sender/provider capacity, load evidence, Kafka/DB/Redis/ClickHouse metrics, retry/DLQ, and observability.
blockedWork.nonGoals: Do not claim throughput for new or unwarmed domains.
acceptanceCriteria: Dated target-like load report exists.
Provider/domain warmup and capacity evidence exists.
Retry, DLQ, suppression, tracking, and monitoring evidence is included.
blockers: External capacity and live load evidence not present in repository
blockedWork.nextAction: Run a target-like load harness with provider-approved capacity before making throughput claims.

blockedWork.title: Add final suppression and preference gate before campaign delivery handoff
blockedWork.scope: Ensure direct and legacy campaign send paths cannot hand off recipients to delivery without authoritative preference and suppression checks, including local audience suppressions and nested email channel preferences.
blockedWork.nonGoals: Do not bypass warmup, rate controls, unsubscribe policy, or provider inbox safety.
acceptanceCriteria: Every campaign send handoff path has a documented final eligibility gate or routes through audience resolution.
Local audience suppressions are included in final eligibility.
Nested channels.email preferences and paused-until preferences are respected.
Focused tests cover direct/legacy send denial for suppression and preference cases.
blockedWork.nextAction: Make a compatibility decision for legacy campaign recipient payloads, then implement the eligibility marker contract if approved.
blockedWork.outcome: Audience-service final eligibility first slice is complete and validated, but the broader parent cannot be marked DONE until a compatibility decision is made for existing campaign legacy recipient JSON payloads. The proposed next slice is an eligibility marker/contract for BatchingService row payloads and SendExecutionService legacy fallback behavior.
residualRisks: Campaign legacy recipient JSON payloads remain a possible bypass until product/operations decide whether old in-flight batches should fail closed, be grandfathered, or be migrated to an eligibility marker.

doneWork.title: Refresh Salesforce and competitor parity matrix
doneWork.scope: Refreshed Salesforce Marketing Cloud and competitor parity sources, updated the dated matrix, and routed implementable gaps into scored backlog candidates.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not make unsupported parity, model-backed AI, production-readiness, inbox-placement, or 10 lakh throughput claims.
acceptanceCriteria: Matrix has dated sources and separates facts from inference.
Gaps are scored and routed to backlog items.
Beginner, advanced, and admin mode implications are recorded without claiming current Admin mode parity.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with the highest-priority READY item: journey-runtime-node-contract.

doneWork.title: Shard Kafka DLQ routing instead of fixed partition zero
doneWork.scope: Shared Kafka listener DLQ routing now preserves failed record source partitions, and the shared/local DLQ topic topology supports six partitions instead of a single hot partition.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not widen Kafka deserialization trusted packages.
acceptanceCriteria: DLQ recoverer no longer routes every failed record to partition 0.
DLQ topic partitioning supports current high-volume source topic partition counts.
Kafka config tests cover source-partition routing and topic partition compatibility.
Local Compose topic initialization is aligned with the shared six-partition DLQ topic.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: salesforce-parity-research-refresh.

doneWork.title: Harden production egress evidence validator
doneWork.scope: Production egress evidence validator now rejects template/example files, placeholder reviewer/rule values, documentation CIDRs, unsupported FQDN evidence, broad/non-canonical CIDRs, future dates, and unsupported schema versions while accepting reviewed public or private endpoint CIDR evidence.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not replace real external egress review with local checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Template evidence fails validation.
Documentation/example CIDRs fail validation.
Placeholder reviewer/provider/rule values and unsupported FQDN evidence fail validation.
Release evidence validator self-test passes with valid public and reviewed private endpoint controls.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: kafka-dlq-sharding.

doneWork.title: Align campaign send handoff with email-send contract
doneWork.scope: Campaign send now always creates and publishes a durable contentReference for email.send.requested while retaining optional inline rendered content only as configured fallback under the payload cap.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not bypass content rendering, sanitization, or tracking URL safety.
acceptanceCriteria: Campaign send handoff always includes contentReference before publishing email.send.requested.
Configured-off and configured-on content-reference tests both emit contract-compliant payloads.
Reference-only payloads remain valid and inline-only or blank-reference shared Kafka payloads are rejected.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: egress-evidence-validator-hardening.

doneWork.title: Make delivery feedback publication durable
doneWork.scope: Added a delivery-local outbox so email sent/failed/bounced/retry feedback is persisted with stable event IDs and retried after Kafka publish failures.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not mark real provider successes failed solely because Kafka is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: Delivery feedback is durably retried via a leased outbox.
Kafka publish failures do not convert accepted provider sends into delivery failures.
Outbox retries reuse stable event IDs, idempotency keys, payloads, and partition keys.
Delivery scheduling is enabled so feedback, retry, and provider health scheduled jobs can run.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: campaign-send-content-reference-contract.

doneWork.title: Replace broad suppression fetch with bulk candidate check
acceptanceCriteria: Audience resolution checks only candidate emails.
Lookup remains tenant/workspace scoped.
Focused audience and deliverability tests cover bulk checks.
Public edge denies child internal suppression routes.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: delivery-feedback-outbox.
doneWork.scope: Add tenant/workspace-scoped bulk suppression lookup for audience resolution candidate emails.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not weaken suppression, unsubscribe, complaint, or tenant isolation behavior.

doneWork.title: Block service-internal endpoints at public edge
doneWork.scope: Prevent public Nginx/ingress exposure of service-to-service `/internal` routes while preserving internal service calls.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not remove controller-level internal token validation or break service-to-service calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Public edge denies known `/internal` routes.
Route map, Nginx, and ingress validators pass.
Service-to-service token validation remains intact.
doneWork.nextAction: Monitor route drift through `scripts\ops\validate-route-map.ps1`.

doneWork.title: Run fresh repository production-readiness audit
doneWork.scope: Run a fresh audit from current repository sources and validators; produce current risks and first implementation slices.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not claim production readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: Current findings cite source files or commands.
Findings are converted into READY, BACKLOG, or BLOCKED queue items.
Memory receives only fresh verified facts.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with highest-priority READY implementation item.

doneWork.title: Tenant-scope feature flag by-ID operations
doneWork.scope: Add authorization and tenant-scoped repository lookup for feature flag get/update/delete by ID.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not introduce global feature flag management behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: GET by ID requires feature read permission.
Get/update/delete by ID use tenant-scoped lookup.
Cross-tenant and missing-tenant cases are tested.
doneWork.nextAction: Monitor for any required global feature flag admin workflow and design it explicitly if needed.

doneWork.title: Align Journey Builder node surface with runtime support
doneWork.scope: Make the frontend Journey Builder node library, backend workflow validation, simulation, and publish/runtime support agree so unsupported nodes cannot be silently published or executed generically.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not build full Salesforce Journey Builder parity or add broad cross-channel runtime features in one slice.
acceptanceCriteria: Every Journey Builder node type exposed in the frontend is either executed by the backend runtime or blocked before publish with a clear validation error.
Backend capability/validation responses and frontend node availability stay synchronized.
Simulation or dry-run output reflects runtime-supported transitions instead of silently treating unsupported nodes as generic next-step movement.
Focused backend and frontend tests cover the node contract and at least one unsupported-node failure path.
validationCommands: cd frontend; npx playwright test tests/e2e/automation-builder.spec.ts --project=chromium
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with the next highest-priority READY item: tracking-ingress-rate-policy.
doneWork.outcome: Journey Builder node creation, activation, backend publish/runtime validation, simulation, and focused tests now share the live runtime-supported node contract. Unsupported known nodes remain draft-only and fail closed before publish or runtime execution.

doneWork.title: Align tracking ingress rate policy with local Nginx
doneWork.scope: Bring Kubernetes tracking ingress rate-limit posture in line with the dedicated high-throughput local Nginx tracking policy.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not claim target throughput without load evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracking ingress has explicit reviewed rate-limit behavior.
Kustomize render and route validation pass.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with the next highest-priority READY item: sso-tenant-cookie-httponly.
doneWork.outcome: Kubernetes ingress now separates signed tracking ingestion from analytics/websocket routes, uses community ingress-nginx limit-rps annotations aligned with local Nginx tracking posture, and route validation enforces the split. This is protective configuration, not throughput evidence.

doneWork.title: Align SSO tenant cookie with normal auth cookie policy
doneWork.scope: Set SSO tenant cookie `httpOnly` behavior consistently with normal auth tenant cookie posture.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not change access or refresh token cookie scope.
acceptanceCriteria: SSO tenant cookie uses the same HTTP-only posture as normal auth.
Focused identity tests cover the SSO callback cookie attributes.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue continuous-cycle backlog refinement; highest-scored backlog candidate is delivery-provider-workspace-isolation, which needs a migration-safe schema plan before implementation.
doneWork.outcome: SSO callback tenant cookies now match normal auth cookie posture: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite, Max-Age, and path assertions are covered for OIDC and SAML ACS callbacks, and failed OIDC callbacks emit no cookies.

doneWork.title: Scope delivery provider configuration by workspace
doneWork.scope: Add workspace isolation to SMTP provider configuration and provider selection.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not edit historical Flyway migrations; add forward migrations only.
acceptanceCriteria: Provider CRUD and selection require tenant plus workspace scope.
Forward migration backfills and constrains workspace ownership safely.
Focused delivery tests cover cross-workspace denial.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue continuous-cycle with production-egress-policy-render-gate; production release remains blocked on external evidence.
doneWork.outcome: Delivery provider configuration is now tenant+workspace scoped across provider CRUD, routing rules, provider selection, orchestration handoff, provider health checks/status, capacity profiles, and failover drills. V16 adds workspace ownership/backfill plus composite provider ownership constraints for routing, pools, scores, health, capacity, and failover tables; focused tests cover cross-workspace denial and migration assertions.

doneWork.title: Prove reviewed production egress policy is rendered and applied
doneWork.scope: Extend strict release validation so reviewed external egress evidence is not only validated but also rendered into the production network policy and included in the target production manifest.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not substitute generated policy proof for real provider/CIDR review or target-environment evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Strict release mode proves reviewed external egress policy generation or manifest inclusion.
Kustomize render includes the reviewed egress policy when strict egress evidence is supplied.
Self-tests cover missing generated policy and stale generated policy failure modes.
doneWork.nextAction: Stop safely per user request after recording; do not start the next item until resumed.
doneWork.outcome: Strict egress release validation now validates reviewed evidence, generates or checks a reviewed external egress NetworkPolicy, proves it renders through a temporary production Kustomize overlay, verifies evidence hash/CIDR/port/protocol inclusion, rejects stale or missing generated policies, rejects FQDN rules until a supported generator exists, and guards the app-label selector against uncovered Deployment pod templates. This does not replace target-environment egress evidence.

doneWork.title: Design AI governance before model-backed optimization features
doneWork.scope: Document and design the governance foundation for AI assistance, send-time optimization, predictive segments, and frequency optimization before claiming true model-backed AI parity.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not add provider credentials, call external model APIs, or claim Salesforce Einstein parity in this slice.
acceptanceCriteria: AI capability claims distinguish deterministic heuristics from model-backed features.
Data-use, provider disclosure, opt-in, human review, audit, metering, and minimum data-threshold requirements are documented.
Backlog follow-ups are split into content assistance, send-time optimization, predictive segments, and frequency optimization.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the highest-scored safe AI follow-up or another unblocked backlog item after integrating six parallel read-only audits.
doneWork.outcome: AI governance foundation documented deterministic-vs-model-backed claim boundaries, tenant/workspace data-use policy requirements, provider disclosure, opt-in/out, audit, metering, human review, minimum data thresholds, fallback behavior, and four follow-up governed AI slices. Enforcement remains future implementation work.
residualRisks: Governance is documented but not enforced in code yet. Model-backed AI claims and provider integration remain blocked until follow-up slices add implementation and tests.

doneWork.title: Scope audience suppression deletion by tenant and workspace
doneWork.scope: Fix suppression soft-delete to require tenant and workspace ownership instead of raw ID lookup, and add focused regression tests for cross-workspace/cross-tenant denial.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not change suppression matching semantics, unsubscribe policy, campaign resolution, or deliverability suppression behavior beyond deletion ownership.
acceptanceCriteria: Suppression delete resolves the row by tenant, workspace, and ID before soft delete.
Known IDs from another tenant or workspace are rejected and remain undeleted.
Focused tests cover same-workspace delete and cross-workspace/cross-tenant denial.
No existing suppression matching or campaign audience resolution behavior is weakened.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next highest-priority safe backlog item after integrating active read-only audits.
doneWork.outcome: Audience suppression delete now requires current tenant and workspace ownership before soft delete; foreign suppression IDs return not found and are not saved.
residualRisks: Other audience governance findings remain open: list-membership segment rules can be skipped and predictive segment governance is not implemented.

doneWork.title: Make audience segment rule evaluation fail closed
doneWork.scope: Fix segment evaluation and service validation so list-membership rules are honored and unsupported fields/operators fail closed instead of being skipped.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not implement full predictive segment governance or frontend builder redesign in this slice; keep UI operator filtering as a follow-up unless backend tests require it.
acceptanceCriteria: IN_LIST and NOT_IN_LIST for list_membership generate tenant/workspace-scoped EXISTS and NOT EXISTS clauses.
Unsupported operators, unsupported fields, and list_membership with non-list operators fail closed with validation errors.
Segment create/update validates rule trees before persistence.
Focused tests cover valid list membership, invalid list membership/operator combinations, and unsupported operator rejection.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next highest-value safe item from parallel audits; likely platform event idempotency or automation live-run confirmation based on P1 blast radius.
doneWork.outcome: Audience segment evaluation now honors tenant/workspace-scoped list membership rules and fails closed for unsupported operators, invalid fields, and invalid list/operator combinations before evaluation or persistence.
residualRisks: Frontend SegmentRuleBuilder still exposes invalid list/operator combinations until a follow-up UX slice filters operators client-side; backend now rejects them fail-closed.

doneWork.title: Require explicit confirmation for Automation Studio live runs
doneWork.scope: Change Automation Studio run requests so empty JSON bodies default to dry-run and live runs require explicit confirmation plus existing ACTIVE/verified/type-supported checks.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not add broad new activity families or arbitrary script execution in this slice.
acceptanceCriteria: An empty JSON run request defaults to dry-run.
Live execution requires explicit confirmation and existing verification/live-supported type gates.
Legacy unsupported ACTIVE activity rows never invoke external clients and record failed runs.
Focused tests cover default dry-run, live confirmation, unsupported live denial, and run listing limits if touched.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next highest-value safe item from refined audits; platform event idempotency and campaign final eligibility gate remain P1 candidates.
doneWork.outcome: Automation Studio run requests now default omitted dryRun to dry-run and require confirmLiveRun=true before any live side-effecting activity execution.
residualRisks: Automation Studio run history remains unbounded and the frontend does not yet render recent run failures; separate follow-up remains needed.

doneWork.title: Add idempotency to platform event consumers
doneWork.scope: Add platform event idempotency before webhook, notification, and search side effects so Kafka retries/replays do not duplicate outbound dispatches or notifications.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not remove existing envelope validation.
acceptanceCriteria: A platform event with an already processed eventId or idempotencyKey returns before webhook, notification, or search side effects.
A failed side effect releases the in-progress claim so Kafka retry/DLQ behavior can reprocess.
Tenant-global events with null workspace dedupe correctly.
Focused tests cover duplicate notification, duplicate webhook, failure release, and idempotency service behavior.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next highest-value safe item from parallel audits; campaign audience eligibility and ClickHouse lineage remain candidates.
doneWork.outcome: Platform event consumers now claim event/idempotency keys before webhook, notification, and search side effects; duplicates return before side effects, side-effect failures release pending claims for retry, and processed-marker failures do not release a completed side effect for duplicate replay.
residualRisks: Target database migration application and Kafka replay behavior still require environment evidence before release claims.

doneWork.title: Preserve experiment lineage in ClickHouse analytics and make rollup refresh idempotent
doneWork.scope: Extend tracking ClickHouse raw event writer/schema for experiment_id, variant_id, holdout, and prevent repeated rollup refreshes from double-counting tenant/workspace/date windows.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not claim AI optimization or winner automation from deterministic experiment analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: ClickHouse raw_events schema and insert path include experiment_id, variant_id, and holdout.
Repeated rollup refresh for the same tenant/workspace/date range cannot double count.
Tests cover lineage insert SQL and idempotent/replace-scoped refresh SQL.
Docs/memory avoid AI-driven optimization claims for this deterministic analytics path.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next highest-value safe item from completed parallel audits; codex lifecycle atomicity, segment UI filtering, and automation run-history paging remain candidates.
doneWork.outcome: ClickHouse raw event schema and writer now preserve experiment_id, variant_id, and holdout; campaign-day rollup refresh deletes the scoped tenant/workspace/date window before insert so repeated refreshes do not double-count.
residualRisks: Target ClickHouse DDL migration/application and high-volume refresh behavior still require environment evidence before release claims.

doneWork.title: Harden Codex lifecycle state mutations for parallel agents
doneWork.scope: Add shared locking/atomic JSON write and preflight ordering around start-work-item, acquire-lease, complete-work-item, and lease validation so parallel agents cannot corrupt state.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not rewrite the autonomous organization model or alter product service code in this slice.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue, lease, thread, checkpoint, and audit mutations are protected by a shared lock or equivalent single-writer guard.
Lease overlap validation happens before invalid leases are persisted.
Failure paths do not leave IN_PROGRESS items without lease/checkpoint evidence.
Disposable temp-copy lifecycle smoke proves promote/start/update/complete/audit validation behavior.
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next safe local item from completed sidecar audits; segment operator filtering and automation run-history paging are ready candidates.
doneWork.outcome: Core Codex lifecycle state mutations now share a named lock and atomic JSON writes; acquire rejects overlapping leases before persistence, start creates lease/checkpoint evidence before queue transition, completion releases leases and thread lease IDs, and temp-copy smoke covers lifecycle behavior.
residualRisks: Less-used cleanup/worktree/module-lease utilities still use older direct write patterns and should be converted in a separate scoped slice if heavy parallel orchestration expands beyond the core lifecycle path.

doneWork.title: Filter segment rule operators in the frontend builder
doneWork.scope: Update SegmentRuleBuilder so list operators are shown only for list_membership and list_membership only shows IN_LIST/NOT_IN_LIST, matching backend fail-closed validation.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not redesign predictive segments or full segment UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Non-list fields cannot select IN_LIST or NOT_IN_LIST in the builder.
list_membership cannot select scalar operators.
Existing saved invalid rules are normalized or surfaced without silent submit.
Frontend lint and targeted UI test pass.
validationCommands: cd frontend; npx playwright test tests/e2e/audience-segments.spec.ts --project=chromium
doneWork.nextAction: Promote the next safe local item from completed sidecar audits; automation run-history paging and send-time optimization governance are ready candidates.
doneWork.outcome: SegmentRuleBuilder now filters operators by field type, normalizes invalid field/operator combinations before submit, and focused Playwright coverage verifies list_membership options and posted payload.
validationRun: PASS: cd frontend; npm run lint
PASS: cd frontend; npm run build:ci
residualRisks: Backend segment validation remains the authoritative fail-closed boundary; the UI still accepts free-text list IDs until a future list picker validates actual list ownership.

doneWork.title: Define governed send-time optimization contract
doneWork.scope: Define send-time optimization readiness thresholds, fallback behavior, confidence/data-quality surfaces, and send-path safety contracts before any model-backed scheduling changes.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not bypass campaign approval, quiet hours, suppression, warmup, provider capacity, rate limits, or deliverability checks. Do not claim throughput or engagement lift without measured evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: STO contract defines minimum data thresholds, lookback windows, low-data fallback, confidence bands, and data-quality reasons.
Commercial and transactional send data are separated by policy.
Scheduling recommendations cannot bypass quiet hours, approvals, suppression, warmup, rate limits, provider capacity, or deliverability gates.
Follow-up implementation tests are identified for campaign and delivery service boundaries.
doneWork.nextAction: Use six read-only discovery results to select the next highest-value safe item.
doneWork.outcome: Implemented deterministic SEND_TIME policy/evaluation governance with data-readiness thresholds, low-data fallback, confidence/data-quality outputs, commercial/transactional data separation, approval/rollback requirements, and launch-timing safety gates; docs updated to avoid model-backed STO or live scheduling claims.
residualRisks: Not model-backed STO; no live scheduling changes.
Campaign/delivery runtime scheduling, timezone behavior, model/data provenance, and target-environment evidence remain future work before STO parity or production claims.

doneWork.title: Expose Automation Studio recent run history in the workspace UI
doneWork.scope: Render recent Automation Studio activity runs from the existing run-history API with compact loading, empty, error, status, dry/live, row-count, timestamp, and error-message states. Refresh visible history after dry-run completion.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not add live-run execution controls, new activity types, arbitrary script execution, external storage credentials, or full orchestration parity.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can expand and collapse recent runs per automation activity.
Run history shows status, dry/live mode, trigger source, rows read/written, started/completed timestamps, and failure message when present.
Expanded history refreshes after a dry-run completes for that activity.
Empty, loading, and error states are visible and scoped to the selected activity.
No live-run button or broader orchestration behavior is added.
doneWork.nextAction: Continue with next safe candidate: release-evidence validator negative fixtures, data-extension governance migration tests, or email governance policy objects.
doneWork.outcome: Implemented compact Automation Studio activity run-history visibility with expandable per-activity panels, run status/dry-live/trigger/row/timestamp/error details, scoped loading/empty/error states, and automatic refresh after dry-run completion.
residualRisks: Backend run-history remains unpaged in this slice.
Richer run trace, dependency ordering, notifications, failure policy, and activity-family execution parity remain under automation-studio-activity-orchestration.

doneWork.title: Add release evidence validator negative fixtures
doneWork.scope: Extend local release evidence validator self-tests with GA and image evidence negative fixtures for placeholder, missing artifact, path escape, absolute path, extra image, missing production image, and digest mismatch cases.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not add real production evidence, read .env or secrets, deploy, push, or claim production readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: GA evidence validator self-test includes a valid manifest pass plus placeholder, missing artifact, absolute path, path escape, and missing required field failures.
Image evidence validator self-test includes valid manifest pass plus missing production image, extra image, digest mismatch, absolute evidence path, and path escape failures.
Self-test uses only temporary local evidence artifacts and deletes them after execution.
Release gate remains local-only and does not mark production evidence complete.

doneWork.title: Add deterministic frequency optimization governance contract
doneWork.scope: Implement deterministic frequency optimization governance evaluation and campaign frequency-cap regression coverage without model/provider integration or live cadence changes.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not call external AI providers, change live send cadence, bypass suppression/unsubscribe/warmup/provider/rate controls, or claim deliverability lift or Salesforce Einstein parity.
acceptanceCriteria: FREQUENCY optimization evaluation is deterministic and exposes lookback, variant/sample readiness, saturation/risk category, recommended cap, confidence band, fallback mode, and safety-impact reasons.
Evaluation fails closed or blocks cadence increases when suppression, unsubscribe, complaint, bounce, warmup, provider, rate-limit, or current frequency-cap signals are unsafe.
Cadence-increase recommendations require human approval and rollback evidence; transactional/commercial and data-quality boundaries remain explicit.
Campaign safety tests prove existing tenant/workspace-scoped frequency caps block sends before delivery handoff.
Docs/product wording avoids model-backed AI, deliverability lift, or production readiness claims.

doneWork.outcome: Added deterministic FREQUENCY optimization policy evaluation and campaign frequency-cap regression coverage without external AI/provider calls or live cadence changes.

doneWork.title: Turn BASIC and ADVANCED UI mode into a workflow contract
doneWork.scope: Promote the existing BASIC/ADVANCED toggle into a reusable workflow metadata and visibility contract, and design role-gated Admin surfaces without claiming current Admin mode parity.
doneWork.nonGoals: Do not hide security-sensitive controls only with CSS or store tokens in browser storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow surfaces define mode metadata instead of relying only on `data-advanced` CSS hiding.
Hidden advanced controls are not keyboard-focusable in BASIC mode.
Admin controls are role-gated and not treated as a third local toggle until implemented.
Playwright or component tests cover mode visibility and direct route behavior.
doneWork.nextAction: Safe-stopped frontend module thread. On resume, render a fresh frontend module prompt, validate state, and choose the next frontend slice such as budget/frequency/template/automation mode metadata.
doneWork.outcome: Added a typed BASIC/ADVANCED frontend mode contract, replaced Settings navigation CSS-only hiding with render-time mode filtering, kept Admin as session-role-gated navigation, and gated the campaign wizard Experiment Engine so BASIC mode neither renders controls nor submits experiment payloads.
residualRisks: Budget, frequency, template command-center, and automation advanced controls still need separate mode-metadata slices with payload/default-policy decisions.
Mode visibility is UX affordance only; backend authorization and safety defaults remain authoritative.
